description: With opposite sides or surfaces close together; not thick or dense. 'Lemi' names the quality of thinness—minimal depth, slender form.
sound_symbolism: The word slips through. 'Le' begins with light ease, substance minimal. Then 'mi' completes delicately, thinness achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'lemi moru' (thin wall). Opposite of 'theru' (thick). Can describe physical thinness or sparse density.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Thin lines created delicacy—fine strokes suggesting lightness and grace.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Thin materials required skill—working with minimal substance demanded precise technique.
